# host_user (Resource)

Manages a local host user without managing its password.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "host_group" "admin" {
  role = "admin"
}

resource "host_group" "developers" {
  name = "developers"
}

resource "host_user" "deploy" {
  username    = "deploy"
  full_name   = "Deploy User"
  shell       = "/bin/zsh"
  create_home = true

  groups = [
    data.host_group.admin.name,
    host_group.developers.name,
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `username` (String) Local username.

### Optional

- `create_home` (Boolean) Create the user's home directory when creating the user. On Linux, changing `home` later moves the home directory when this is true.
- `full_name` (String) User full name/comment. Omit to leave this field unmanaged.
- `groups` (Set of String) Supplementary groups managed by this resource. Groups outside this set are left untouched. Prefer references such as `host_group.admin.name` or `data.host_group.admin.name`.
- `home` (String) User home directory. Omit to use the operating system default and leave this field unmanaged after creation.
- `remove_home_on_destroy` (Boolean) Remove the user's home directory when destroying the resource. Defaults to false.
- `shell` (String) User login shell. Omit to use the operating system default and leave this field unmanaged.

### Read-Only

- `gid` (String) Numeric primary group ID as reported by the operating system.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `username`.
- `uid` (String) Numeric user ID as reported by the operating system.
